pages.raw_text:
In regard to the suggestion of a
citizens investigation into the FL
lott case and police conditions in
general, d. C. Coates, chairman Of
the committee of fifteen of the
Central Labor council, appointed
to take charge of the campaign for
police matrons, today said:


81 am heartily in favor of this
plan, as it has already met with
the approval of the trades council.
We started out in the beginning to
have an investigation by disinter-
ested parties, and I still believe
this ought to be done.


"The findings of the mayors po-
lice investigation confirm the
stand of the Womans club and the
labor unions. The committee, after
hearing only the police side of the
controversy, and examining only
witnesses under the domination of
the police, find that matrons are
necessary. This is an admission
that fully substantiates all that
those asking for matrons have con-
tended from the beginning.


There should be no let up in
the investigation of police condi-
tons until the whole matter is sift-
ed to the bottom, and l will do
everything within my power to aid
in the work"
